summary: Crystal‑clear soundboard capture of a 1989 Frost show with standout China > Rider, Pocky Way and a fiery Ship of Fools.
review: The recording is an excellent soundboard mix – crisp, well balanced and virtually free of drop‑outs, letting the band’s 1989 energy shine through.  The performance is highlighted by Brent Mydland’s vibrant keyboards (especially on Pocky Way), a smashing China > Rider close to set one and a soaring Ship of Fools solo, while a few moments such as Jack Straw feel flat.  Overall the show is a solid, high‑energy Dead concert that rewards repeated listening.

ai_rating.rationale: The vast majority of reviews (13/15) are 5‑star and praise both the sound quality and musical highlights; only a single detailed review is mixed, yielding an overall strong positive assessment.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Crisp, clear mix with rich highs and solid lows; occasional slight muddiness at the very start of the show but no major drop‑outs or distortion.

show_quality.setlist_flow: First set builds nicely and closes with a memorable China > Rider; second set is jam‑heavy, moving from energetic Pocky Way through solid Ship of Fools into tight transitions like Playing in the Band → Eyes of the World.

band_member_comments.Jerry: Garcia delivers standout solos (Ship of Fools, Pocky Way) but slips on lyrics early (Jack Straw) and isn’t always soaring.
band_member_comments.Phil: Bass is clear and driving; several reviewers note Phil’s tone shines in the broadcast mix.
band_member_comments.Keys: Brent Mydland is praised for vibrant Hammond work, especially on Pocky Way and his overall keyboard presence.
band_member_comments.Drums: The rhythm section stays tight; drum‑to‑space transitions are noted as energetic but receive no specific criticism.
